Title: JA Regulates Caffeine Biosynthesis in Tea Leaf for Resistance Against Fungal Infection and Antagonises With GA to Balance the Defence–Growth Trade-Off via CsDELLA-JAZ-MYC2-MYB184-TCS1 Module

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockdown; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling; promoter transactivation assay; EMSA; fungal infection assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated by fungal infection and MeJA; associated with young leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by fungal infection and MeJA; repressed by GA and DIECA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defence; contributes to resistance against Colletotrichum gloeosporioides
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Young leaves; mesophyll cells; epidermal cells; cuticle
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes caffeine biosynthesis and tea resistance to fungal infection
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum gloeosporioides infection; MeJA; DIECA; GA3
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native TCS1 promoter in reporter assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher caffeine is associated with defence and may participate in growth–defence trade-off context
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering elevated leaf caffeine and fungal resistance, but should be balanced with growth traits under JA-GA crosstalk
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper experimentally uses TCS1 as the output biosynthetic gene controlled by CsMYB184/JAZ/DELLA/MYC2 module rather than as the main mechanistic focus enzyme step
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TCS1 expression is induced by fungal infection and MeJA, paralleling caffeine accumulation. 2. TCS1 is directly activated by CsMYB184 and repressed indirectly by CsJAZ proteins. 3. Downregulation of TCS1 decreases caffeine accumulation and fungal resistance.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes bioactive gibberellins GA4 and GA1 into inactive GA34 and GA8, respectively
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts hormone pool away from active GA, indirectly promoting caffeine biosynthesis and defence while inhibiting growth
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for GA inactivation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves and stems; induced by fungal infection and MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by fungal infection and JA; activated by CsMYC2; repressed by CsJAZ12
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Caffeine supports antifungal defence; CsGA2ox4 contributes indirectly through JA-GA trade-off regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GA inactivation, inhibits plant growth, and supports defence-associated caffeine biosynthesis indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; LC-MS; overexpression in tobacco; hormone profiling; EMSA; transient promoter activation assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CsGA2ox4 promoter in transient LUC assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth inhibition and dwarf phenotype due to reduced bioactive GA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to strengthen defence by reducing GA activity, but likely incurs growth penalty and should be tuned conditionally
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a caffeine pathway enzyme, it is a core pathway enzyme in the hormonal mechanism establishing the growth–defence trade-off linked to caffeine accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsGA2ox4 converts GA4 to GA34 and GA1 to GA8 in vitro. 2. Its overexpression reduces bioactive GA and causes dwarfism. 3. JA/CsMYC2 activate CsGA2ox4, linking defence signalling to GA inactivation and caffeine-associated defence.

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and/or cytoplasm in interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signalling; fungal infection; CsMYC2 activation; repression by CsJAZ1/CsJAZ12/CsJAZ13
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of JA-induced amplification loop with CsMYC2 and JAZ repressors
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms inhibitory complexes with CsJAZ1; CsJAZ12; CsJAZ13; competes with CsDELLA1/2 for CsJAZ12 association indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by MeJA; correlated with JA signalling genes during leaf development; associated with young leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by MeJA and reduced by DIECA or CsMYC2 knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tea leaves, especially young leaves and cuticle-associated tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key positive regulator that promotes caffeine biosynthesis and fungal resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; GUS/LUC transactivation; EMSA; antisense knockdown; metabolite profiling; fungal infection assay; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsMYC2; CsJAZ1; CsJAZ12; CsJAZ13; CsDELLA1; CsDELLA2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key activator of caffeine biosynthesis in tea leaves
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes defence-associated caffeine accumulation within JA-GA growth–defence balance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to enhance caffeine-mediated disease resistance in tea, though deployment should consider growth suppression under JA-associated states
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its N-terminus binds TCS1 promoter, whereas C-terminus mediates transactivation and JAZ interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB184 directly binds the TCS1 promoter and activates its transcription. 2. MeJA induces CsMYB184, and CsMYC2 directly activates its promoter. 3. JAZ proteins repress CsMYB184 activity, while DELLAs relieve this repression through JAZ competition.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: bHLH transcription factor; JA signalling effector
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Forms JA signalling amplification loop by activating CsMYB184; contributes to defence-growth trade-off via CsGA2ox4
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with JAZ proteins including CsJAZ12 (reported in text/supporting discussion)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Correlated with CsMYB184 and CsJAZ expression during leaf development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functionally active under MeJA and fungal infection; knockdown reduces CsMYB184, TCS1 and caffeine
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antifungal defence; also linked to growth inhibition through GA inactivation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes caffeine biosynthesis and defence, while suppressing growth via activation of GA catabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; promoter activation assays; antisense knockdown; transient overexpression; transgenic Arabidopsis phenotyping; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major JA effector linking hormone signalling to both caffeine accumulation and GA-mediated growth repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth inhibition, reduced hypocotyl elongation, elevated GA inactivation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for inducible defence engineering, but constitutive activation may penalize growth; best used with stress-inducible strategies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly binds G-box motifs in promoters of CsMYB184 and CsGA2ox4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYC2 directly activates CsMYB184 to induce caffeine biosynthesis. 2. CsMYC2 also activates CsGA2ox4 to promote GA inactivation and growth repression. 3. Knockdown of CsMYC2 decreases CsMYB184, TCS1, and caffeine levels.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ protein with ZIM and Jas domains
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and/or cytoplasm in interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of JA repression arm that is relieved during JA activation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves; induced by fungal infection; peaks in mature leaves according to discussion
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of caffeine biosynthesis and fungal resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; EMSA competition; transient repression assay; combined asODN knockdown; hairy root overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a direct repressor of the key caffeine activator CsMYB184
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Relieving repression may strengthen defence but may alter JA-growth balance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockdown/editing may enhance caffeine-based resistance, but redundancy with other JAZs suggests multiplex targeting is preferable
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The N-terminus of CsJAZ1 mediates interaction with the C-terminus of CsMYB184
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsJAZ1 physically interacts with CsMYB184. 2. CsJAZ1 suppresses CsMYB184 binding/activation of TCS1. 3. Reduction of JAZ activity increases caffeine and fungal resistance.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ protein with ZIM and Jas domains
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and/or cytoplasm in interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses CsMYB184-mediated TCS1 activation and suppresses CsMYC2 activation of CsGA2ox4
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: JA signalling; fungal infection; antagonized by CsDELLA1/2
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Central repression node in JA-GA antagonistic loop
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms inhibitory complexes with CsMYB184 and interacts with CsDELLA1/2; also antagonizes CsMYC2 activity on CsGA2ox4
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in leaves and correlated with caffeine; induced by fungal infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major negative regulator of caffeine biosynthesis and positive growth regulator through limiting JA outputs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; EMSA competition; transient repression assay; asODN knockdown; hairy root and leaf overexpression; Arabidopsis overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary JAZ node integrating JA and GA signals to control caffeine output
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression promotes growth traits but lowers caffeine and disease resistance; knockdown enhances defence
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime multiplex editing target to release caffeine biosynthesis, though excessive derepression could impair growth balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in the paper as the principal JAZ partner for DELLA-mediated JA-GA crosstalk
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsJAZ12 binds CsMYB184 and represses TCS1 activation. 2. CsJAZ12 suppresses CsMYC2-driven CsGA2ox4 activation and promotes growth-related GA status. 3. CsDELLA1/2 competitively bind CsJAZ12 to relieve repression of caffeine biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ protein with ZIM and Jas domains
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and/or cytoplasm in interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves; induced by fungal infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-enriched and developmentally correlated with caffeine
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of caffeine biosynthesis and fungal resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; EMSA competition; transient repression assay; combined asODN knockdown; hairy root overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: One of the main JAZ repressors dampening caffeine output in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Relief of repression may enhance defence at potential growth cost in JA contexts
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for stacked editing with CsJAZ1/CsJAZ12 to elevate caffeine-based defence
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts similarly to CsJAZ1/12 on CsMYB184, but DELLA-crosstalk emphasis is mainly on CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsJAZ13 physically interacts with CsMYB184. 2. CsJAZ13 represses CsMYB184 binding and activation of TCS1. 3. JAZ knockdown elevates caffeine and disease resistance.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DELLA protein with DELLA and GRAS domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA-JA crosstalk affecting caffeine biosynthesis and growth
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsJAZ12-CsMYB184 complex indirectly; TCS1 expression indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Relieves CsJAZ12 repression on CsMYB184, thereby promoting TCS1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GA status; JA-associated GA inactivation via CsGA2ox4
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in antagonistic JA-GA loop stabilised by GA inactivation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with CsJAZ12 and other JAZs; competitively interferes with CsJAZ12-CsMYB184 complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves where caffeine is enriched
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of caffeine biosynthesis and fungal resistance; repressor of growth/GA signalling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in tobacco and tea hairy roots; asODN knockdown; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; EMSA competition; LUC assay; fungal infection assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key GA-side activator of caffeine biosynthesis through relief of JAZ repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf phenotype and growth repression when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be leveraged to boost defence under stress, but constitutive enhancement likely penalizes growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The GRAS-domain-containing C-terminus mediates interaction with CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDELLA1 is a canonical GA signalling repressor causing dwarfism when overexpressed. 2. CsDELLA1 promotes TCS1 expression and caffeine accumulation. 3. CsDELLA1 competitively binds CsJAZ12 to release CsMYB184 activity.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DELLA protein with DELLA and GRAS domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA-JA crosstalk affecting caffeine biosynthesis and growth
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsJAZ12-CsMYB184 complex indirectly; TCS1 expression indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Relieves CsJAZ12 repression on CsMYB184, thereby promoting TCS1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GA status; JA-associated GA inactivation via CsGA2ox4
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in antagonistic JA-GA loop stabilised by GA inactivation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with CsJAZ12 and other JAZs; competitively interferes with CsJAZ12-CsMYB184 complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves where caffeine is enriched
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of caffeine biosynthesis and fungal resistance; repressor of growth/GA signalling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in tobacco and tea hairy roots; asODN knockdown; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; EMSA competition; LUC assay; fungal infection assay
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key GA-side activator of caffeine biosynthesis through relief of JAZ repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dwarf phenotype and growth repression when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential defence-enhancing regulator but likely unsuitable for constitutive activation because of growth penalty
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like CsDELLA1, its GRAS-domain-containing C-terminus mediates interaction with CsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsDELLA2 is a canonical GA signalling repressor causing dwarfism when overexpressed. 2. CsDELLA2 promotes TCS1 expression and caffeine accumulation. 3. CsDELLA2 competitively binds CsJAZ12 to release CsMYB184 activity.
